headline: AI Goes From Pilot to Production—Agentic Systems Are the New Battleground

stories.title: Agentic AI Is Reshaping How Brands Interact With Consumers
stories.summary: Brands gotta adapt to AI agents handling product research & purchases—tbh moving from chat copilots to full AI-to-AI interactions is lowkey changing retail forever. Three modes emerging: brand agents, personalized third-party agents, or AI negotiating with other AI on your behalf.
stories.takeaway: Companies leaning into proprietary customer data + experimentation will win the agentic commerce race. It's not just buying software—it's ongoing prompt optimization across whole AI ecosystems.

stories.title: AI Adoption Challenges Are Actually About People, Not Tech
stories.summary: McKinsey found orgs struggling to scale gen AI—but fr, it's not technical problems. It's experiential ones. People gotta actually want to use these tools, which means designing for human behavior, not just capability.
stories.takeaway: The real competitive edge comes from making AI tools people embrace, not forcing adoption. Workflow redesign + human trust > model quality.

stories.title: Enterprises Moving to Multi-Agent Orchestration—Not Just Single Copilots
stories.summary: a16z just dropped that 2026 is the year AI stops being isolated tools and starts coordinating like actual teams. Agents managing complex workflows together means you need new 'systems of coordination' on top of legacy systems of record.
stories.takeaway: The real disruption: traditional CRM/ITSM systems lose primacy as autonomous execution layer takes over. Infrastructure + governance > raw capability.

stories.title: Voice AI Agents Will Dominate Customer Service in 2026
stories.summary: CB Insights says multimodal ground is where the customer service AI land grab happens—voice is the proving ground bc it's unforgiving (latency matters, handoffs suck). Agents nailing voice + seamless text/image/doc extension will win.
stories.takeaway: Customer service is #1 AI agent adoption area. Companies like PolyAI proving it's not about audio modality—it's barge-in handling, silence detection, and turn-taking built into architecture from day one.

watchNext.title: AI Brain Fry Is Real—And Companies Need to Address It
watchNext.why: Programmers describing 'buzzing' feelings & mental fog from coordinating swarms of AI agents. Steve Yegge's Gas Town shows the productivity paradox: faster execution lowkey creates more cognitive load. Enterprise leaders sleeping on the human cost angle.

watchNext.title: Generative Coding Is Becoming Enterprise Standard—With Real Security Risks
watchNext.why: MIT's 10 Breakthrough Technologies list included generative coding fr. Microsoft writes 30% of code with AI, Google writes 25%+. But Veracode found 45% of AI outputs hide known vulnerabilities. Security reviews aren't optional—they're table stakes.
